description_long: This is a simple wooden fence constructed from three horizontal rails and two vertical posts, with an additional diagonal brace running from the lower left to the upper right for structural support. The wood appears to be smooth and uniformly colored, with a medium brown finish that suggests it is either stained or painted. The posts and rails are rectangular in cross-section, and the diagonal brace is composed of the same material, giving the fence a cohesive look. There are no visible decorative elements or embellishments, and the top of the fence is flat and unadorned. The fence is solid and functional, likely intended to demarcate boundaries or restrict passage in an urban or suburban setting. The construction is robust, but the overall design is utilitarian rather than ornamental.
description: A simple, medium-brown wooden fence with three horizontal rails, two posts, and a diagonal brace for support. The design is flat-topped and unadorned, emphasizing function over decoration.
description_view_0: Front view showing three horizontal rails, two posts, and a diagonal brace running from bottom left to top right.
description_view_1: Side view showing the thin profile of the fence, with the posts and rails aligned in a single plane.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with the diagonal brace now running from bottom right to top left due to perspective.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin profile and the alignment of the posts and rails.
